name: Chuck Faranda Globular Cluster Workflow
description: Optimized for globular clusters with emphasis on resolving dense stellar cores while preserving natural star colors.

steps.note: Crop only stacking artifacts. Preserve faint outer cluster members.
Remove pedestal without clipping the background.
Preferred: AutoBGE. Alternatives: GraXpert AI or VeraLux Nox. Preserve faint field stars.
Neutralize background while maintaining natural stellar color.
Preferred: SPCC. Verify realistic stellar color distribution.
RC-Astro BlurXTerminator. Preferred: BlurXTerminator (15–25%) then NoiseXTerminator (very light). StarXTerminator is generally NOT recommended. Alternatives: SyQon Parallax and SyQon Prism.
RC-Astro NoiseXTerminator. Apply conservatively for the target.
RC-Astro StarXTerminator. Use only when the workflow calls for starless processing.
Preferred: Generalized Hyperbolic Stretch using gradual stretches to avoid core saturation.
Enhance contrast conservatively while preserving stellar color.
Optional. Apply lightly only to improve dense core separation.
Save the 32-bit floating-point FITS working master. Create a 16-bit TIFF only for final publication, printing, or software that requires TIFF.

author: Chuck Faranda
